title: Exploring the anti-glioma mechanism of the active components of Cortex Periplocae based on network pharmacology and iTRAQ proteomics in vitro
author: Che
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The active components of  Cortex Periplocae  (CP) exhibit anti-tumor properties in many cancers. However, litter is known about its effect and the underlying mechanism on glioma. The study investigated the underlying mechanism of CP for treating glioma. U251 and TG905 cells were treated with the ethanol extract of CP. Cell proliferation was detected by CCK8 and colony formation assay. Flow cytometric analysis was applied to explore the induction of cell cycle arrest and apoptosis. The expression levels of cell cycle- and apoptosis-associated proteins were measured by western blot. Network pharmacology method was performed to predict the potential mechanism of the active components of CP on glioma. Then, iTRAQ-based quantitative proteomics analysis was used to verify the differentially expressed proteins and pathways to reveal the underlying mechanisms. Furthermore, to determine the iTRAQ results, 6 candidate proteins were chosen for quantification by PRM. The extract of CP inhibited the proliferation of U251 and TG905 cells and induced the cell cycle arrest and apoptosis. There are 16 active compounds of CP. The anti-tumor mechanism of CP may be related to apoptosis pathway, p53 signaling pathway, PI3K-AKT pathway and transcriptional misregulation in caner pathway. And 6 proteins (HSP90AB1, TOP2A, ATP1A1, TGFβ1, ATP1B1 and TYMS) are considered as the key factors involved in the regulation of CP to glioma. Our research revealed the underlying mechanism of CP for treating glioma by integrated network pharmacology and iTRAQ-based quantitative proteomics technology.
